scope.summary: Implement GarmentModelAdapter trait extension point: a named adapter registry entry that routes TailorModelAdapter calls to a specialized garment foundation model (AIpparel-style) via a registered model_id, alongside the general LlmClient path, selectable per workspace or per call
acceptance_criteria: TailorModelAdapter gains a model_id: Option<String> field in AuthorGarmentInput; when model_id is None the default workspace LlmClient is used

The kernel model-lane registry is extended with a GarmentFoundationModel variant alongside the existing LlmClient adapters; the variant carries a model_endpoint: String and a model_schema: String identifying the expected input/output format
When model_id resolves to a GarmentFoundationModel entry, TailorModelAdapter routes the CompletionRequest to that model's endpoint using the existing LlmClient trait interface (no new HTTP client)
The GarmentSpec output from any registered model must pass through the identical fast-validation and sandbox lifecycle regardless of which model produced it

A tailor_model_adapter_registry table (dated migration) stores registered garment model entries keyed by model_id, with fields: model_id TEXT PK, endpoint TEXT, schema_version TEXT, is_default BOOL, workspace_id TEXT
ModelManual documents how to register a specialized garment model and how model_id routing works
proof_targets: cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::garment_model_registry_lookup (model_id resolves to a GarmentFoundationModel entry from tailor_model_adapter_registry)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::garment_foundation_model_output_validated (output from mocked garment foundation model passes fast validation before draft creation)
cargo test -p handshake_core tailor::model_adapter::default_llm_used_when_no_model_id (None model_id routes to default workspace LlmClient)
scope.risk_if_missed: When specialized garment foundation models (AIpparel and successors) mature and outperform general LLMs for pattern generation, Tailor cannot adopt them without a code change to TailorModelAdapter. The parallel-swarm moat depends on being able to swap or combine model backends per workspace without changing the API surface. Without this registry, adopting a new garment model requires a Tailor release, breaking the model-agnostic design contract.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
